title: Missing 14-year-old Manitoba girl found dead, police say
full_text:
Missing 14-year-old girl last seen in Lake Francis, Man., found dead, RCMP say
Teen's death being investigated by Stonewall RCMP, officials from chief examiner's office
A 14-year-old girl who was last seen in a community northwest of Stonewall on Thursday afternoon has been found dead, Manitoba RCMP said on Saturday.
The teen had been reported missing to Stonewall RCMP from an address on Government Road in Lake Francis, about 65 kilometres northwest of Winnipeg, on Thursday, police said in an earlier news release.
She had last been seen at 3:30 p.m. that day, when she was seen on social media in a field where her family's horses are kept, police said.
She wasn't home when her father and brother returned to the residence in the evening, police said. Her cellphone wasn't on, her friends hadn't seen her, and she wasn't found during a search of the property or the community.
Police said she was found dead in an update on Saturday morning.
Her death is being investigated by Stonewall RCMP officers and officials from the chief medical examiner's office.
CBC News has removed the teen's name and image to respect personal privacy now that she has been found.
